mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer spending without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises alongside CPIAUCSL increases exceeding 0.3 percent monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization and near-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of recovery-phase observations, or the predictive power vanishes when applied outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue bases, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the signal yields negative returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of restrictive monetary policy and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL monthly changes exceed 0.4 percent, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal underperforms individual proxies, or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS rises while UNRATE also increases.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in housing starts signal deteriorating construction activity and reduced household wealth effects, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of instances following two consecutive monthly housing starts declines, or the signal loses predictive power outside late-cycle environments.
